items.prompt: interprets ratio language as a multiplicative relationship between two quantities
items.expected_evidence: explain statements such as 2 to 3 or 3 for every 5 as coordinated multiplicative relationships
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner interprets and represents the ratio correctly.

items.prompt: represents simple ratios in words, diagrams, tables, and symbolic ratio forms
items.expected_evidence: match verbal, pictorial, tabular, and symbolic ratio representations in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains how the forms describe the same ratio relationship.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to additive_comparison_substitution.
items.expected_evidence: reject additive interpretations when a task is ratio-based
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the additive mistake and states the multiplicative relationship.

items.prompt: succeeds across part-to-part and part-to-whole ratio contexts without collapsing them into the same claim
items.expected_evidence: compare simple ratio situations using multiplicative rather than difference-based reasoning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner succeeds in a different ratio context without collapsing relationship type.
